3503 total tracks in database, 1984 tracks with at least one sale

pi_items.verified_value: 70.0 - Interpreted as: remaining revenue percentage < 70% (cumulative revenue > 30%)
pi_items.source: Algorithm in export_result.sh: WHEN (RunSum - Rev) * 1.0 / Total < 0.70 THEN 'A'

pi_items.verified_value: 90.0 - Interpreted as: remaining revenue percentage < 90% (cumulative revenue > 10%)
pi_items.source: Algorithm in export_result.sh: WHEN (RunSum - Rev) * 1.0 / Total < 0.90 THEN 'B'

pi_items.verified_value: Yes - table exists with columns: InvoiceLineId, InvoiceId, TrackId, UnitPrice, Quantity

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Chinook sample database (v1.4.5), a well-known public SQLite database containing real iTunes media data (3503 tracks, 275 artists, 347 albums) with fictitious customer records. The database has 1984 tracks with at least one sale, generating total revenue of 2328.6. The ABC inventory classification algorithm categorizes tracks by remaining cumulative revenue percentage: A (top items, 351 tracks, 30.57% of revenue), B (70-90% threshold, 0 tracks - empty due to data distribution), and C (bottom items, 1633 tracks, 69.43% of revenue). The categorization boundary occurs at track rank 351, where remaining revenue percentage jumps from 30.49% directly to 99.96%, creating an empty B category.
